name_en: Set of Tableware from the Azuchi-Momoyama Period
description_en: A complete set of tableware from the Azuchi-Momoyama period, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 1978-02-01. Held at the Tome City History Museum, Hasama-chō Sanuma aza Uchimachi 63-20, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Long Sword (Tachi)
description_en: A single long sword (tachi), classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 1984-09-20. Held at the Tome City History Museum, Hasama-chō Sanuma aza Uchimachi 63-20, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Short Sword (Wakizashi) Forged by Date Tsunamune
description_en: A short sword (wakizashi) forged by Date Tsunamune, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 2015-03-23. Held at the Tome Kaikokan, Toyoma-machi Teraike Sakurakōji 72-6, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Long Sword (Tachi)
description_en: A long sword (tachi), classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 2015-03-23. Held at the Tome Kaikokan, Toyoma-machi Teraike Sakurakōji 72-6, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Short Sword (Wakizashi)
description_en: A short sword (wakizashi), classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 2015-03-23. Held at the Tome Kaikokan, Toyoma-machi Teraike Sakurakōji 72-6, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Uchigatana Sword Mounting
description_en: An uchigatana sword mounting (koshirae), classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 2015-03-23. Held at the Tome Kaikokan, Toyoma-machi Teraike Sakurakōji 72-6, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Western-Style Dagger Mounting
description_en: A Western-style dagger mounting (koshirae), classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 2015-03-23. Held at the Tome Kaikokan, Toyoma-machi Teraike Sakurakōji 72-6, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Sword by Arai Sōryūshi Gyokuei
description_en: A sword by the swordsmith Arai Sōryūshi Gyokuei, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 2005-01-26. Held at the Tome Kaikokan, Toyoma-machi Teraike Sakurakōji 72-6, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Commentaries on the Classic of Poetry, in Thirteen Volumes
description_en: A thirteen-volume set of commentaries on the Classic of Poetry (Mōshi shūso), classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 1978-03-28. Held at the Tome City History Museum, Hasama-chō Sanuma aza Uchimachi 63-20, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Tōkai Gyoshō, One Scroll
description_en: A single scroll of the Tōkai gyoshō, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 1978-03-28. Held at the Tome City History Museum, Hasama-chō Sanuma aza Uchimachi 63-20, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_romaji: Sendai-han ni okeru Kyōhō kaikaku-ji no kankei monjo
name_en: Documents Relating to the Kyōhō Reforms in the Sendai Domain
description_en: A collection of documents relating to the Kyōhō-era reforms in the Sendai domain, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 1972-08-28. Held at the Tome City History Museum, Hasama-chō Sanuma aza Uchimachi 63-20, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Personal Letter of the Kōmon Lord
description_en: A personal letter (shinkan) of the Kōmon lord, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 1978-09-26. Held at the Tome Kaikokan, Toyoma-machi Teraike Sakurakōji 72-6, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_romaji: Date Masamune hitsu koka "Saku toki wa"
name_en: Old Poem "Saku toki wa" in the Hand of Date Masamune
description_en: A classical poem beginning "Saku toki wa," written in the hand of Date Masamune, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 2015-03-23. Held at the Tome Kaikokan, Toyoma-machi Teraike Sakurakōji 72-6, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_romaji: Sanuma-jō ato naka no itabi ikki
name_en: One Stone Stupa from the Sanuma Castle Site
description_en: A single itabi stone stupa from the Sanuma Castle site, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 1978-02-01. Located at Shikagajō Park, Hasama-chō Sanuma aza Uchimachi 12, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_romaji: Boshin no eki Tome hanshi kishōmon
name_en: Written Oaths of Tome Domain Retainers from the Boshin War
description_en: Written oaths (kishōmon) of Tome domain retainers from the Boshin War, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 1978-09-26. Held at the Tome Kaikokan, Toyoma-machi Teraike Sakurakōji 72-6, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Grand Council of State Public Notice Board
description_en: A public notice board (kōsatsu) issued by the Grand Council of State (Dajōkan), classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 1995-02-27. Held at the Byōdōnuma Rural Culture and Nature Learning Center, Toyosato-chō Kusuda 64-1, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Materials Relating to Temmon Gorobei
description_en: Materials relating to Temmon Gorobei, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is municipally designated by the city of Tome, designated on 2023-08-25. Held at the Tome City History Museum, Hasama-chō Sanuma aza Uchimachi 63-20, Tome, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Golden Dragon and Chinese Lions with Peonies
description_en: A carving depicting a golden dragon and Chinese lions with peonies, classified as a tangible cultural property (sculpture). It is municipally designated by the city of Tomiya. Located at Yusenji, Nezakizawa, Tomiya.

name_en: Stone Statue of Lord Masamune on Horseback
description_en: A stone statue depicting Lord Date Masamune on horseback, classified as a tangible cultural property (sculpture). It is municipally designated by the city of Tomiya. Located at Atago Shrine, Yaguradshita, Tomiya.

name_romaji: Yakushi Nyorai zō, Niō zō, Jūni Shinshō zō
name_en: Statues of Yakushi Nyorai, the Niō Guardians, and the Twelve Heavenly Generals
description_en: Statues of Yakushi Nyorai, the Niō guardian kings, and the Twelve Heavenly Generals, classified as a tangible cultural property (sculpture). It is municipally designated by the city of Tomiya. Located at Ochiai Yakushidō, Tomiya.

name_en: Stone Basin of Ōshimizu
description_en: A stone basin at Ōshimizu, classified as a tangible cultural property (sculpture). It is municipally designated by the city of Tomiya. Located at 1-chome Kamisakuragi.

name_en: Sakaki-ryū Perpetual Kagura Dance
description_en: A perpetual kagura sacred dance of the Sakaki school, classified as a folk cultural property (intangible folk cultural property). It is municipally designated by the city of Tomiya. Located in the Ōkame district.

name_en: Amatsu-ryū Nambu Kagura Dance
description_en: A Nambu-style kagura sacred dance of the Amatsu school, classified as a folk cultural property (intangible folk cultural property). It is municipally designated by the city of Tomiya. Located in the Imaizumi district.

name_en: Turtle Stone
description_en: The Kameishi (turtle stone), classified as a monument (historic site). It is municipally designated by the city of Tomiya. Located at Amatarawake Shrine, Ōkame Wagōda 2-ban, Tomiya.

name_en: Magistrate's Pine
description_en: The Daikan matsu (magistrate's pine tree), classified as a monument (natural monument). It is municipally designated by the city of Tomiya. Located at the Tomiya Central Community Center, Nishizawa, Tomiya.

name_en: Turtle Cedar
description_en: The Kame sugi (turtle cedar tree), classified as a monument (natural monument). It is municipally designated by the city of Tomiya. Located at 2-chome Takanomori Park.

name_en: Asian Skunk Cabbage Colony
description_en: A colony of mizubashō (Asian skunk cabbage), classified as a monument (natural monument). It is municipally designated by the city of Tomiya. Located at Ninosekihara Zaike-yama.

name_en: Konpōji Temple Bell (Kanbun-Era Bell)
description_en: The temple bell of Konpōji, known as the Kanbun-era bell, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is town-designated by Wakuya, designated on 2018-07-02. Located at Nonodake aza Kagurraoka, Wakuya, Tōda District, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Gravestones of the Wakuya Date Family
description_en: A group of gravestones of the Wakuya Date family, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is town-designated by Wakuya, designated on 2012-02-08. Located at Wakuya aza Ryūenji, Wakuya, Tōda District, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Children's Burial Ground of the Wakuya Date Family
description_en: The children's burial ground of the Wakuya Date family, known as the Okosama-byō, classified as a tangible cultural property (fine arts and crafts). It is town-designated by Wakuya, designated on 2018-07-02. Located at Wakuya aza Ryūenji, Wakuya, Tōda District, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Kenryūbyō Mausoleum (Spirit Hall within the Wakuya Date Family Cemetery)
description_en: The Kenryūbyō spirit hall within the Wakuya Date family cemetery, classified as a tangible cultural property (building). It is town-designated by Wakuya, designated on 1971-05-13. Located at Wakuya aza Ryūenji, Wakuya, Tōda District, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Yakuimon Gate of the Sengoku Family
description_en: The yakuimon gate of the Sengoku family, classified as a tangible cultural property (building). It is town-designated by Wakuya, designated on 1972-05-08. Located at Wakuya aza Shimomachi, Wakuya, Tōda District, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Myōkengū Shrine (Worship Hall and Main Hall)
description_en: The Myōkengū shrine, comprising its worship hall (nagatoko) and main hall (honden), classified as a tangible cultural property (building). It is town-designated by Wakuya, designated on 1986-12-22. Located at Wakuya aza Hinatamachi, Wakuya, Tōda District, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Yakuimon Gate of Saikōji Temple
description_en: The yakuimon gate of Saikōji, classified as a tangible cultural property (building). It is town-designated by Wakuya, designated on 1993-01-18. Located at Osato aza Fudō, Wakuya, Tōda District, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_romaji: Sasaki-ke jūtaku (hiroma, omoya, itagura ni-tō, munamon, kyūsha)
name_en: Sasaki Family Residence (Hall, Main House, Two Plank Storehouses, Gate, and Stable)
description_en: The Sasaki family residence, comprising its great hall, main house, two plank storehouses, ridged gate and stable, classified as a tangible cultural property (building). It is town-designated by Wakuya, designated on 2000-06-28. Located at Wakuya aza Koganeoi, Wakuya, Tōda District, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Corner Turret of the Wakuya Fort Site (Drum Tower)
description_en: The corner turret of the Wakuya fort site, known as the drum tower (taikodō), classified as a tangible cultural property (building). It is town-designated by Wakuya, designated on 2000-06-28. Located at Wakuya aza Shimomachi, Wakuya, Tōda District, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Koganeyama Shrine (Worship Hall)
description_en: The worship hall (haiden) of Koganeyama Shrine, classified as a tangible cultural property (building). It is town-designated by Wakuya, designated on 2005-03-03. Located at Wakuya aza Koganemiyamae, Wakuya, Tōda District, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Priests' Quarters of the Doi Family (Niō Hall)
description_en: The priests' quarters of the Doi family, known as the Niō hall (Niōdō), classified as a tangible cultural property (building). It is town-designated by Wakuya, designated on 2005-03-03. Located at Nonodake aza Kagurraoka, Wakuya, Tōda District, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Hakusan Hall and Niō Gate of Konpōji Temple
description_en: The Hakusan hall and Niō gate of Konpōji, classified as a tangible cultural property (building). It is town-designated by Wakuya, designated on 2018-07-02. Located at Nonodake aza Kagurraoka, Wakuya, Tōda District, Miyagi Prefecture.

name_en: Lion Dance Votive Tablet
description_en: A votive tablet (ema) depicting a lion dance, classified as a folk cultural property (other). It is town-designated by Wakuya, designated on 2000-06-28. Located at Wakuya aza Hinatamachi, Wakuya, Tōda District, Miyagi Prefecture.
